description: An ALTER that falls back to ALGORITHM=COPY locks the table and stalls all writes for the rebuild.
body: Failure mode: an ALTER TABLE that cannot run inplace silently uses ALGORITHM=COPY, building a shadow table under a metadata lock so every write queues behind it for the full copy duration. Detection signals: the processlist fills with Waiting for table metadata lock, application write latency spikes, and SHOW PROCESSLIST shows the ALTER copying to tmp table. Do not run an unguarded ALTER on a large hot table during peak traffic. Safer fallback: declare ALGORITHM=INPLACE, LOCK=NONE so the statement fails fast if it cannot run online, and use gh-ost or pt-online-schema-change in a scheduled window for true copy operations.
